group_label: CYTOTOXIC CD8+ T CELL / NK CELL
group_name: CYTOTOXIC CD8+ T CELL / NK CELL
source_rationale: CD8A confirms CD8+ T cell identity, while GNLY, GZMB, GZMH, PRF1, NKG7, and FGFBP2 indicate a highly cytotoxic effector phenotype. KLRD1 (CD94) is shared between cytotoxic T cells and NK cells.

GNLY, GZMB, GZMK, and PRF1 indicate strong cytotoxicity. KLRC1 (NKG2A) is an NK receptor, CRTAM marks recently activated lymphocytes, CSF2 (GM-CSF) indicates activation, and XCL2 is characteristic of NK/ILC1 cells.

FCGR3A (CD16) suggests non-classical monocytes, while CLEC10A and CD1C-absence but S100A9 and IL1B indicate an inflammatory myeloid cell. FPR2 and HES4 are characteristic of non-classical monocytes, with EREG and SMIM25 supporting a DC-monocyte transitional state.

C1QA, LYVE1, and SELENOP are canonical markers of tissue-resident macrophages, particularly Kupffer cells or perivascular macrophages. RNASE1, IGF1, and CCL2 further support a homeostatic tissue-resident macrophage identity.

group_label: MESENCHYMAL STROMAL CELL / HEPATIC STELLATE CELL
group_name: MESENCHYMAL STROMAL CELL / HEPATIC STELLATE CELL
source_rationale: PDGFRA marks mesenchymal cells, while GPC3 is highly expressed in hepatic tissue. CXCL12, CXCL14, IGF1, CSF1, and BMP4 indicate a supportive stromal cell with niche-signaling functions, consistent with hepatic stellate cells or periportal fibroblasts.

FOXP3 is the master transcription factor of Tregs. CTLA4, TIGIT, and HPGD are canonical Treg markers. CCR6 and BATF suggest an effector Treg phenotype, while CD28 and CD2 confirm T cell lineage.

ACTA2 (alpha-smooth muscle actin) and RGS5 are canonical markers of pericytes/myofibroblasts. POSTN (periostin) indicates an activated fibrotic phenotype, while BMP2 and CCL2 suggest tissue remodeling activity.

CXCR6 is a hallmark of tissue-resident memory T cells. GZMK and PRF1 indicate cytotoxic capacity, CCL5 is characteristic of effector memory T cells, and KLRC1 (NKG2A) with CSF2 suggest an activated tissue-resident phenotype.

CLEC9A, XCR1, and CADM1 are the most specific markers of cDC1s. IRF8 is the master transcription factor for cDC1 development, and WDFY4 is required for cross-presentation. DPP4 (CD26) further confirms cDC1 identity.

FCER1A (high-affinity IgE receptor alpha) is expressed on basophils and mast cells. FCGR2B (CD32B) is an inhibitory Fc receptor. This minimal marker set with FCER1A as the dominant feature is most consistent with basophils.

FCER1A (high-affinity IgE receptor alpha) is expressed on basophils and mast cells. FCGR2B (CD32B) is an inhibitory Fc receptor. This minimal marker set with FCER1A as the dominant feature is most consistent with basophils.

CSF3R (G-CSF receptor) is the canonical neutrophil growth factor receptor. FCAR (CD89, IgA Fc receptor) is highly expressed on neutrophils. S100A8 and SELL (CD62L) further confirm a neutrophil identity.

LAMP3, CCR7, FSCN1, and EBI3 are hallmarks of mature migratory DCs. CCL17, CCL19, CCL22 indicate chemokine-producing capacity, while IRF1, IRF4, and CD1C suggest a matured cDC2-derived DC. IL-15 and TNFSF13B support immune-regulatory function.

CD1C and CLEC10A are canonical cDC2 markers. FCER1A, IRF4, and CEBPB are key cDC2 transcription factors/receptors. MRC1 (CD206) and CD14 suggest a monocyte-like cDC2 subset (DC3), with CADM1 and TNFSF9 supporting dendritic cell identity.

CD1C and CLEC10A are canonical cDC2 markers. FCER1A, IRF4, and CEBPB are key cDC2 transcription factors/receptors. MRC1 (CD206) and CD14 suggest a monocyte-like cDC2 subset (DC3), with CADM1 and TNFSF9 supporting dendritic cell identity.
